channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Officials demand answers as crews work to restore power after another Puerto Rico blackout
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Crews worked early Thursday to restore power to Puerto Rico after a blackout that hit the entire island affected the main international airport, hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ukraine's future is at the center of talks in Paris with Rubio, Witkoff and top European officials
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France hosted a series of talks Thursday about Ukraine and its security, including U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio and presidential envoy Steve Witkoff and top Ukrainian and European officials. After hours of separate meetings at the French presidential palace with U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration takes aim at Harvard's international students and tax-exempt status
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration has escalated its ongoing battle with Harvard, threatening to revoke the university's ability to host international students as the president called for withdrawing Harvard’s tax-exempt status.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, JOCELYN GECKER and COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Undelivered: Two weeks with no mail for some Virginia residents
item.description: Issues at a Virginia post office have caused some residents in Suffolk to go nearly two weeks without mail delivery.
item.dc:creator: Jimmy LaRoue, NewsNation

item.title: Over 150 dogs rescued from 'extremely poor' conditions inside home
item.description: 152 dogs were rescued from a home in Ogden, Utah, where they were reportedly living in "extremely poor and overcrowded" conditions, according to the Ogden Police Department. Lt.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, KTLA 5

item.title: Kamala Harris holds lead in hypothetical California governor race, poll shows
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris would enjoy a wide lead over the rest of the field if she were to enter the 2026 race for California governor, the results of a recent poll show.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Officials in Puerto Rico demand answers after another blackout leaves 1.4 million without power
item.description: Crews worked early Thursday to restore power to Puerto Rico after a blackout across the entire island that affected the main international airport, several hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, WFLA, NBC

item.title: China dominates solar. Trump tariffs target China. For US solar industry, that means higher costs
item.description: President Donald Trump's escalating trade war with China means hopeful solar buyers may have a much harder time getting systems installed in the U.S. in coming months.
item.dc:creator: Alexa St. John, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DA files motion to delay Menendez brothers' resentencing hearing
item.description: The resentencing hearing for Eric and Lyle Menendez, scheduled for Thursday morning, could be delayed due to a motion filed by Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman. Hochman filed the motion Wednesday, saying he wants time for the court to review a parole assessment ordered by Gov.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: New York family told missing woman was found dead, only to learn she's living in Detroit
item.description: A New York family is navigating an emotional journey after learning their sister, whom officials declared dead last year, is actually alive and living out of state.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Garcia, ABC 4

item.title: Frontier Airlines passenger describes terrifying landing after plane’s wheel breaks off
item.description: A Frontier Airlines flight lost a wheel during a terrifying landing at Luis Munoz Marin International Airport in Puerto Rico on Tuesday, a passenger said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police records expose 'violent' behavior of deported alleged MS-13 gang member and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Noem announces cancellation of $2.7 million in grants to Harvard
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Wednesday announced the cancellation of more than $2.7 million in grants to Harvard University amid tensions between the school and the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans consider increasing taxes on the rich in break from party orthodoxy
item.description: Republicans in Congress are considering increasing taxes on the rich as a part of President Trump’s “big, beautiful bill” of ambitious legislative priorities, a striking development that breaks with decades of party orthodoxy and is spurring alarm bells from traditional conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, ABC 4

item.title: San Francisco's new mayor is starting to unite the fractured city
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Democrats nationally may be in turmoil, but liberals in San Francisco are hopeful the new mayor's collaborative approach will help solve entrenched problems in a city recently known for its bitter infighting and chaotic streets.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Noem announces cancellation of $2.7 million in grants to Harvard
item.description: “Harvard bending the knee to antisemitism — driven by its spineless leadership — fuels a cesspool of extremist riots and threatens our national security,” Noem said in the release.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Conservation group names Mississippi River 'most endangered,' cites proposed FEMA cuts
item.description: American Rivers, a nonprofit environmental advocacy group, said the Mississippi River in recent years has faced “increasingly frequent and severe floods” that have damaged homes and businesses and worsened the health of the river, which provides drinking water for 20 million people.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: ACLU sues Defense Department school system over banning race- and gender-related books
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union has sued the Department of Defense’s school system for children of military families, asserting that the removal of race- and gender-related books and curricula violated students’ First Amendment protections against government censorship.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wink Martindale, the genial game show host and an early TV interviewer of Elvis Presley, dies at 91
item.description: Wink Martindale, the genial host of such hit game shows as “Gambit” and “Tic-Tac-Dough” who also did one of the first recorded television interviews with a young Elvis Presley, has died. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: IRS agent who investigated Hunter Biden expected to be named IRS acting chief
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — An IRS whistleblower who testified publicly about investigations into Hunter Biden’s taxes is expected to be promoted to IRS acting commissioner, two sources familiar with the plan tell The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fort Benning takes back its old name, but to honor a different soldier
item.description: The Army is restoring the name Fort Benning to its storied training post in Georgia, only this time to honor an 18-year-old corporal who fought in World War I rather than a Confederate general. A ceremony to make the name change official was scheduled Wednesday at the base just outside Columbus.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON and RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Harvard stands to lose $2.2 billion in federal funding. Researchers fear science will suffer
item.description: CAMBRIDGE, Mass. (AP) — In a high-stakes standoff, President Donald Trump's administration says it will freeze $2.2 billion in federal research grants for Harvard University, which is pushing back on demands for changes to campus policy.
item.dc:creator: BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Santa Fe sheriff releases body camera footage of Gene Hackman investigation
item.description: Newly-released body camera footage shows the confusion and questions investigators had after actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were found dead in their home.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Video shows ICE agents shatter car window during Massachusetts arrest: attorney
item.description: Federal immigration agents are accused of shattering a car window in Massachusetts while detaining a Guatemalan man on Monday in a caught-on-camera incident – despite his attorney saying they had the wrong person.
item.dc:creator: Jusolyn Flower, Fox 5

item.title: Actor William Levy arrested after drunken 'disturbance' at Florida restaurant: report
item.description: Cuban-American actor William Levy was arrested in South Florida Monday after causing a "disturbance" inside a restaurant, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man allegedly held captive by stepmother in Connecticut releases first public statement
item.description: In his first public statement, the man allegedly held captive in his home for over 20 years by his stepmother says he's been getting stronger as he begins the process of reclaiming his life.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, WGN 9

item.title: Pixar Putt will be swinging into Riverton with family fun mini golf experience
item.description: Fans of "Toy Story", "The Incredibles", and more Pixar classics, get ready for a fun family activity coming to Riverton.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Strong geomagnetic storm could bring northern lights to multiple states, including Illinois: forecast
item.description: Skywatchers across several states may have the chance to see the northern lights on Tuesday as a coronal mass ejection impacts Earth.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Gov. Cox declares 'Declutter Day' in Utah
item.description: The Utah Division of Consumer Protection and Gov. Spencer J. Cox declared April 18, 2025 as the official Declutter Day in Utah.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Go inside the factory where Peeps are made
item.description: BETHLEHEM, Pa. (AP) — Love them or hate them, those marshmallow Peeps that come in blindingly bright colors and an array of flavors are inescapable around the Easter holiday.
item.dc:creator: TASSANEE VEJPONGSA and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal judge drops contempt case against ICE agent over arrest outside Boston courthouse
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge has dismissed a contempt case against an agent for the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement whose arrest of a man during his trial sparked criticism among the law enforcement community in Boston.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jeffries: Court should hold Trump officials in contempt over wrongly deported Maryland man
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) said Tuesday that the Supreme Court should hold members of the Trump administration in contempt of court if they don’t move to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported last month to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9

item.title: Biden says Trump administration has done 'breathtaking' damage to Social Security
item.description: Former President Joe Biden warned a Chicago audience assembled Tuesday at the national conference of Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled that the Trump administration has "taken a hatchet" to Social Security.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Watch: Elephants at San Diego park form protective circle during earthquake
item.description: Watch: Elephants at the San Diego Zoo Safari Park surrounded the younger elephants in a protective "alert circle" when a 5.2 magnitude earthquake struck the region Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stocks rally worldwide after Trump eases some of his tariffs on electronics, for now
item.description: Stocks rose worldwide Monday after President Donald Trump relaxed some of his tariffs, for now at least, and as stress from within the U.S. bond market seems to be easing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Search continues for Las Vegas veterinarian who apologized for kicking horse
item.description: Earlier this month, Dr. Frehner apologized for kicking a horse he was hired to give shots to. Days later, he was reported missing in the Lake Mead Recreation Area.
item.dc:creator: Madina Ansary, Fox 5
